steps.title: Compare the two views
steps.content: Text 1 says Okoro's plays are best understood chronologically, in three cohesive periods. It presents this grouping as a useful way to handle the vast body of work. Text 2 argues that imposing that linear three-phase structure can overlook commonalities across phases and differences among plays from the same time.

steps.title: Avoid invented contrasts
steps.content: Choice B invents a claim that Okoro's style stayed consistent. Choice C switches to poetry, which neither text discusses. Choice D invents a disagreement about which plays are most political. The real contrast is usefulness versus limitation. The answer is A.
